perturbation.original_text: The initial term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year from the Effective Date (the "Initial Term"). This Agreement will automatically renew for additional 1-year terms unless earlier terminated pursuant to this Agreement's express provisions (together with the Initial Term, such additional periods the "Term").
perturbation.changed_text: The initial term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year from the Effective Date (the "Initial Term"). This Agreement will renew for additional terms as Customer agrees to in writing, but does not require termination notice to prevent auto renewal (together with the Initial Term, such additional periods the "Term").
perturbation.explanation: By changing "This Agreement will automatically renew for additional 1-year terms unless earlier terminated pursuant to this Agreement's express provisions" to "This Agreement will renew for additional terms as Customer agrees to in writing, but does not require termination notice to prevent auto renewal" a contradiction is being introduced in section 4. The original text implies that the agreement auto-renews, unless terminated according to terms in 4b. The modified text now requires explicit written agreement from Customer for any renewals but doesn't require termination notice. This in-text contradiction introduces uncertainty about the renewal process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The initial term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year from the Effective Date (the "Initial Term"). This Agreement will renew for additional terms as Customer agrees to in writing, but does not require termination notice to prevent auto renewal (together with the Initial Term, such additional periods the "Term"). Neither Party may terminate this Agreement during the Initial Term except as set forth in paragraph 4(b) or any Exhibit. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason following the Initial Term upon ninety (90) days written notice to the other Party. Except for termination for material breach as detailed in paragraph 4(b), Kubient shall be entitled to all fees generated during the termination period regardless of which Party terminates the Agreement
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the renewal terms. Originally, the agreement automatically renewed unless terminated. The revised text requires explicit written agreement from the Customer for renewal, removing the automatic renewal aspect. The 'Contradicted Text' is the same as the 'Changed Text', and the contradiction lies in the initial assumption of auto-renewal versus the need for explicit agreement. This creates a direct conflict regarding how the agreement is renewed, fulfilling the criteria for a structural flaw leading to an in-text contradiction, as defined in the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will protect the Confidential Information, for one (1) year from the date of disclosure, by using at least the same degree of care as it uses to protect its own Confidential Information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent unauthorized use, disclosure or publication.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will protect the Confidential Information indefinitely from the date of disclosure, by using at least the same degree of care as it uses to protect its own Confidential Information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent unauthorized use, disclosure or publication.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Recipient will protect the Confidential Information, for one (1) year from the date of disclosure" to "Recipient will protect the Confidential Information indefinitely from the date of disclosure" an in-text contradiction is being introduced in section 12. The beginning of section 12, "Recipient will protect the Confidential Information indefinitely from the date of disclosure", implies there is no set term, while a few sentences later it modifies this claim in the following clause "excluding information that:(a) was in Recipient's possession before receipt from the Discloser pursuant this Agreement; (b) is in or enters the public domain without a breach of this Agreement; (c) is rightfully received by Recipient from a third party who was not known by Recipient to be legally or contractually restricted from disclosing such information; or (d) is independently developed by Recipient without use of or reference to the Discloser's Confidential Information. Recipient will protect the Confidential Information, for one (1) year from the date of disclosure". This in-text contradiction introduces uncertainty about the Confidentiality terms duration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Confidential Information" means any tangible and intangible non-public information in any form (including written information, oral statements and electronically stored data) which a party discloses (the "Discloser") to the other party (the "Recipient") including, without limitation, information relating to trade secrets, systems, know-how, products, processes (including manufacturing processes), inventions, computer software programs, marketing or sales techniques, financial condition, costs, business interests, initiatives, objectives, plans, strategies, customers, suppliers, lenders, underwriters, or employees, that is marked as confidential or identified at the time of disclosure as being confidential or is otherwise disclosed under circumstances that would lead a reasonable person to conclude that such information is confidential, excluding information that: (a) was in Recipient's possession before receipt from the Discloser pursuant this Agreement; (b) is in or enters the public domain without a breach of this Agreement; (c) is rightfully received by Recipient from a third party who was not known by Recipient to be legally or contractually restricted from disclosing such information; or (d) is independently developed by Recipient without use of or reference to the Discloser's Confidential Information. Recipient will protect the Confidential Information indefinitely from the date of disclosure, by using at least the same degree of care as it uses to protect its own Confidential Information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent unauthorized use, disclosure or publication.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original confidentiality period from 'one (1) year' to 'indefinitely'. The contradicted text, which now contains this modification, states 'Recipient will protect the Confidential Information indefinitely from the date of disclosure' and then later includes the clause 'Recipient will protect the Confidential Information, for one (1) year from the date of disclosure'. This creates a direct in-text contradiction regarding the duration of the confidentiality obligation. The change introduces uncertainty and conflicting terms, as the obligation appears both indefinite and limited to one year. This violates the Structural Flaws definition.

perturbation.original_text: Customer shall be responsible for paying all applicable sales, use or other taxes, duties, or tariffs applicable to its use of the Services.
perturbation.changed_text: Customer shall only be responsible for paying sales taxes, and Kubient is responsible for all use or other taxes, duties, or tariffs applicable to its use of the Services.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Customer shall be responsible for paying all applicable sales, use or other taxes, duties, or tariffs applicable to its use of the Services" to "Customer shall only be responsible for paying sales taxes, and Kubient is responsible for all use or other taxes, duties, or tariffs applicable to its use of the Services" a contradiction is being introduced in section 5. The original text implies that Customer is responsible for all taxes due to the service, and the modified text now states Customer is only responsible for sales taxes, creating an in-text contradiction. This introduces uncertainty about which party is responsible for what taxes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree to share any revenue generated as a result of this Agreement, or Customer's use of the Services or the Auction Platform, as set forth and detailed in each applicable Exhibit. Unless specified otherwise in an Exhibit, Kubient shall be responsible for contracting with all advertisers, agencies, media buyers, and/or sponsors (collectively "Advertisers") on Customer's behalf, including Advertisers with whom Customer has existing relationships. Pursuant to the doctrine of sequential liability, payment from Kubient to Customer shall be made within seven (7) days from Kubient's receipt of payment, regardless of when Customer submits an invoice. Customer acknowledges and agrees that Kubient shall proceed with recovery of the amounts due on a best effort basis but will not initiate any legal action against a delinquent party without the express written consent and support of Customer. Except of taxes associated with Kubient's income, Customer shall only be responsible for paying sales taxes, and Kubient is responsible for all use or other taxes, duties, or tariffs applicable to its use of the Services.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the customer is responsible for all taxes, but the changed text limits the customer's responsibility to only sales taxes, assigning the rest to Kubient. This directly contradicts the text within the 'Contradicted Text' section, where it says 'Customer shall only be responsible for paying sales taxes, and Kubient is responsible for all use or other taxes, duties, or tariffs applicable to its use of the Services.' Since the Contradicted Text section explicitly states the same information as the Changed Text section, there is a contradiction with the original text within the same section, as the original text would cause the Customer to be responsible for all taxes. This creates a direct conflict regarding tax responsibilities, leading to a strong in-text contradiction, as defined under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
